LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANT ANN CAMERON TO FURTHER RESPOND TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET THREE; AND TO PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS AND THINGS RESPONSIVE THERETO - THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM HALPERIN. IN SUMMARY, THAT RECOMMENDATION PROVIDES AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED, IN PART, AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 1 (THE INVESTIGATIVE FILE CONCERNING THE HARASSMENT CLAIM IN THE SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE), AND ANY DESCRIPTIONS OF MS. CHASE'S DUTIES THAT MAY BE FOUND IN HER EMPLOYMENT FILE. DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION AND SANCTIONS. FOR REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 1, THE RESPONDING PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO PRODUCE REDACTED COPIES WHERE THE CLAIMANT'S AND INVESTIGATION TARGET'S NAMES REDACTED AND PRODUCTION TO OCCUR PURSUANT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER. FOR MS. CHASE'S FILE, DESCRIPTIONS OF MS. CHASE'S DUTIES THAT MAY BE FOUND IN HER EMPLOYMENT FILE ARE DIRECTLY RELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS THAT PLAINTIFF'S TERMINATION PURSUANT TO THE REDUCTION IN FORCE WAS A PRETEXT. HOWEVER, PRODUCTION OF OTHER MATERIALS IN THE EMPLOYMENT FILE ARE NOT BE JUSTIFIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.). JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING DEFENDANT NORTHERN TRUST, NA TO FURTHER RESPOND TO DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET THREE; AND TO PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS AND THINGS RESPONSIVE THERETO - THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM HALPERIN. IN SUMMARY, THAT RECOMMENDATION PROVIDES AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED, IN PART, AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 1 (THE INVESTIGATIVE FILE CONCERNING THE HARASSMENT CLAIM IN THE SAN FRANCISCO OFFICE), AND ANY DESCRIPTIONS OF MS. CHASE'S DUTIES THAT MAY BE FOUND IN HER EMPLOYMENT FILE. DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION AND SANCTIONS. FOR REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NO. 1, THE RESPONDING PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO PRODUCE REDACTED COPIES WHERE THE CLAIMANT'S AND INVESTIGATION TARGET'S NAMES REDACTED AND PRODUCTION TO OCCUR PURSUANT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER. FOR MS. CHASE'S FILE, DESCRIPTIONS OF MS. CHASE'S DUTIES THAT MAY BE FOUND IN HER EMPLOYMENT FILE ARE DIRECTLY RELEVANT TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS THAT PLAINTIFF'S TERMINATION PURSUANT TO THE REDUCTION IN FORCE WAS A PRETEXT. Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Appendix Of Non-California Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Barbara I. Anonucci, Esq., from Littler Mendelson, P.C., 415-288-6358, for defendants and moving party
Allen J. Kent, Esq., Law Office, 415-257-4700, for plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and tentative ruling is adopted.
DEFENDANT NORTHERN TRUST, NA'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER.
Motion granted, without prejudice to Plaintiff re-noticing Ms. Barrat's deposition at a later date. Under Liberty Mutual Ins. Co. v. Sup.Ct., (1993) 10 Cal.App.4th 1282, before a deposition of a corporate president or other high level corporate official can be taken it must be established that the deponent has unique or superior personal knowledge of discoverable information. If not, Plaintiff must first obtain the necessary discovery through less intrusive methods including interrogatories or depositions of lower level employees or the corporation itself. Should these avenues be exhausted, and Plaintiff makes a colorable showing of good cause that the high-level official possesses necessary information to the case, the deposition can go forward. In this instance, Ms. Barrat states that she was not involved in determining that Plaintiff should be one of the individuals that was terminated during a reduction in force. Plaintiff's primary reason for taking Ms. Barrat's deposition is to determine whether her performance reviews of Steven Bell criticized Bell for his handling of Plaintiff's termination. At this juncture, it's speculative whether Ms. Barrat ever criticized Mr. Bell for his handling of Plaintiff's dismissal. Additionally, it appears that Ms. Barrat's performance review of Mr. Bell has recently been produced to Plaintiff and Mr. Bell has yet to be deposed. Under Liberty Mutual, Plaintiff must first exhaust other methods of discovery before proceeding with Ms. Barrat's deposition. Plaintiff is not precluded from ever deposing Ms. Barrat, only that Plaintiff must exhaust other discovery first, and adduce evidence that contradicts Ms. Barrat's declaration. Since Plaintiff has made a reasonable argument in opposition to the motion (though ultimately not successful,) the request for sanctions is denied. The Kent declaration submitted in opposition to the motion will be ordered sealed since it contains confidential information. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. (Prevailing party to submit a form of order.) Pro Tem Judge: David McDonal; Not reported
